articles.title: NEXT-LEVEL FROSTED GLASS WITH BACKDROP-FILTER
articles.summary: This article shows how to create a highly optimized "frosted glass" effect using CSS `backdrop-filter` and `mask-image`. The standard `backdrop-filter: blur()` only blurs elements directly behind the target, so the author introduces a technique using a larger masked element to include nearby content in the blur. This solution addresses issues like unclickable elements (using `pointer-events: none`) and flickering at the viewport edges (using a background gradient).

articles.title: REACT DATA FETCHING PATTERNS
articles.summary: This article explores common data fetching patterns in React applications, focusing on how data is fetched within components. It focuses on sequential fetching (where data fetches depend on each other) and parallel fetching (where multiple fetches happen concurrently using `Promise.all`). It also goes over prefetching data to improve perceived performance.

articles.title: I DON'T KNOW HOW TO BUILD SOFTWARE AND YOU DON'T EITHER
articles.summary: The long-term consequences of architectural decisions don't become apparent for several years. This makes it nearly impossible to accumulate enough experience to definitively answer questions like microservices vs monoliths. Personal opinions are often shaped by early career experiences.

articles.title: WHAT IS AI ENGINEERING?
articles.summary: AI engineers are different from ML engineers in that they focus more on MLOps and data integration. Generally, AI engineers build AI systems that solve real business problems using pre-trained models and advanced techniques like Agentic RAG.

articles.title: FAILED AND FLAKY TESTS SLOWING DOWN YOUR MERGES?
articles.summary: Flaky tests are incredibly frustrating — as is digging through CI logs to find which line of code caused a test failure. Use Codecov Test Analytics [8] to separate flaky tests from legitimate failures, highlight code that led to failed tests, and improve your test suite over time with detailed stats. articles.title: WHAT'S NEW IN SVELTE: DECEMBER 2024
articles.summary: Svelte's December 2024 update includes Svelte 5 bug fixes, CLI improvements, automatic type declarations, and more support from several UI libraries like Shadcn, Zag, and Skeleton.

articles.title: KLONGPY
articles.summary: KlongPy is a high-performance Python implementation of the Klong array language that leverages NumPy for vectorized operations. It supports CPU and GPU backends. KlongPy seamlessly integrates with the Python ecosystem for data analysis and distributed computing applications.

articles.title: SEEKSTORM
articles.summary: SeekStorm is an open-source, high-performance, sub-millisecond full-text search library and multi-tenancy server written in Rust with a focus on low latency and scalability. It offers features like faceted search, geo-proximity search, and real-time indexing.

articles.title: THE BEAUTIFUL MATH OF BLOOM FILTERS
articles.summary: Bloom filters are probabilistic data structures that efficiently check if an element exists within a large dataset. They achieve this by using an array of bits and multiple hash functions to map elements to bit positions - a set bit suggests presence, while an unset bit definitively indicates absence. Bloom filters can produce false positives (incorrectly indicating presence), but filters' size and number of hash functions can be optimized through mathematical analysis, minimizing this probability.

articles.title: C++, COMPLEXITY, AND COMPILER BUGS
articles.summary: C++ has some inherent complexities which are often due to subtle language features like temporary lifetime extension and designated initializers, which vary across different compiler versions. These inconsistencies and unexpected behaviors, sometimes only discovered after extensive debugging and consultation with language experts, show the large amount of time and effort required to work with the language effectively.

articles.title: FACEBOOK'S LITTLE RED BOOK
articles.summary: Now publicly available digitally, Facebook's internal "Little Red Book” is a manifesto preserving its founding ethos and illustrating how storytelling scales culture during rapid growth.

articles.title: DIFF TEXT
articles.summary: Paste two chunks of text and find the difference in lines, words, or characters.

articles.title: STARTING OFF RIGHT: WHERE AUTOFOCUS SHINES
articles.summary: The `autofocus` attribute in HTML improves user experience on single-purpose pages like login forms by automatically focusing the first input field.

articles.title: RUST SOLVES THE ISSUES WITH EXCEPTIONS
articles.summary: Rust's error handling, unlike exception-based systems, provides a more robust and predictable approach by explicitly handling errors as values.

articles.title: INTEL ANNOUNCES RETIREMENT OF CEO PAT GELSINGER
articles.summary: Intel's CEO Pat Gelsinger has retired - David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus were named interim co-CEOs, with Holthaus also appointed CEO of Intel Products.
